title: Optimizing React Memoized Component Re-renders on Array Reference Change

statement.markdown: A common use case for memoization in React is to prevent unnecessary re-renders of components based on changing props. However, when dealing with arrays, a subtle bug can occur when an array reference changes, but the array contents remain the same. In this challenge, you are given a MemoizedComponent that re-renders unnecessarily due to changing array references. Your task is to fix the bug by implementing a correct way to compare array references.
statement.inputFormat: An array of integers as a string
statement.outputFormat: Whether the component should re-render or not
statement.notes: You should not use the `React.memo()` function or any external libraries.

constraints.description: The number of integers in the array
The number of times the array reference is updated

examples.explanation: The array reference is updated 5 times, but the array contents remain the same, so the component should not re-render
The array reference is updated once, so the component should re-render

hints.text: Think about how to compare array references correctly

editorial.title: Optimizing React Memoized Component Re-renders on Array Reference Change
editorial.intuition: Comparing array references is not as straightforward as comparing array contents.
editorial.bruteForce: Use the `JSON.stringify()` function to compare array references, but this can be slow for large arrays.
editorial.betterSolution: Use a custom comparison function that checks if the array references are the same.
editorial.optimalSolution: Use the `===` operator to compare array references directly.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The `===` operator checks if the two references point to the same object in memory.
editorial.dryRun: Try running the code with different input values and see how the component re-renders.
commonMistakes: Not using a custom comparison function
